disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sustained excessive tachycardia on standing (rise well over 30 bpm, up to ~140) that resolves on lying down, with presyncope
trigger_question_examples: What happens to your heart rate when you stand vs lie down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Lying down my heart's about 75, but within a minute of standing it shoots up to 130-140 and I feel like I'll faint - it settles the moment I lie back down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blood pressure stays stable (even rises slightly) on standing - no orthostatic hypotension
trigger_question_examples: Do you know what your blood pressure does on standing?
Does it drop when you stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My blood pressure actually stays about the same or goes up a bit when I stand - it's the heart rate that goes crazy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dependent acrocyanosis (reddish-purple discolouration) of the legs on standing, resolving on lying down
trigger_question_examples: Do your legs change colour when you stand?
Tell me about the purple discolouration.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: After a few minutes standing my legs and feet go reddish-purple, and it clears when I lie down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic fatigue, severe exercise intolerance, tremulousness and 'brain fog'
trigger_question_examples: Any fatigue, brain fog or shakiness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm exhausted all the time, can't exercise, feel shaky, and my thinking is foggy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No true syncope (blackout), and the tachycardia does not persist while lying flat (arguing against a primary arrhythmia)
trigger_question_examples: Does the racing continue when you're lying flat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I haven't actually blacked out, and the racing settles once I'm lying flat.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: My last doctor said it was just anxiety. Is that all this is?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should validate that this is a real, objective problem - a big, reproducible heart-rate rise on standing with the legs changing colour and immediate relief on lying down points to a disorder of the autonomic nervous system (POTS), not simply anxiety - while explaining the diagnosis is confirmed with standing (active-stand/tilt) testing and managed with fluids, salt, compression and gradual reconditioning.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Do I need to go to hospital for this?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should reassure that POTS, though debilitating, is not immediately life-threatening and usually does not need emergency care; recommend aggressive fluids and salt, waist-high compression, a shower chair and counter-manoeuvres now, arrange GP/specialist (autonomic/cardiology) review, and give safety-netting for true syncope or a fast heart rate persisting while lying flat.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may have been dismissed as anxious or may push for emergency assessment - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises POTS, validates it, starts conservative management and refers, without over-triaging.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: Maybe it really is just in my head like they said.
